content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

August 22, 1996

R. Thomas Harris
City Attorney
425 North El Dorado Street
Stockton, CA 95202

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-96-232

Dear Mr. Harris:

You have requested advice on behalf of Councilmember Ted
Gonzales concerning the conflict-of-interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Section 83114 provides that
any person may request an opinion or written advice from the
Commission with respect to his or her own duties under the Act.
FPPC Regulation 18329 (copy enclosed) establishes the procedures
under which the Commission issues written advice including to
persons with a duty to advise other persons relating to their
duties or actions. (Section 18329(c)(1).)

However, your request for advice is too hypothetical and
appears to request assistance concerning the duties of persons
other than Councilmember Gonzales. Pursuant to subsection (c)(4)
of Regulation 18329, we decline to provide written advice to you
at this time. However, I am enclosing copies of past advice
letters that may answer your questions.

If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Julia Butcher
Graduate Student Assistant,
Legal Division

Enclosures

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. Commission regulations
appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000-18995.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: Pursuant to subsection (c)(4) of Regulation 18329, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. However, I am enclosing copies of past advice letters that may answer your questions.
sections.question_synthetic: Councilmember Ted Gonzales sought advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, submitted through the City Attorney.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the request was too hypothetical and appeared to seek assistance concerning the duties of persons other than Councilmember Gonzales, though past advice letters were provided as a reference.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The question and conclusion were extracted from the body text. The document indicates past advice letters were enclosed but not included in this text.

QUESTION: Councilmember Ted Gonzales sought advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, submitted through the City Attorney.

CONCLUSION: Pursuant to subsection (c)(4) of Regulation 18329, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. However, I am enclosing copies of past advice letters that may answer your questions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to issue written advice to the City Attorney on behalf of Councilmember Gonzales regarding Political Reform Act conflict-of-interest provisions, citing that the request was too hypothetical and concerned duties of persons other than the direct requestor.
